title: Build a Real-World Multi-Agent AI App with MCP + LangGraph | Part 2
summary: This tutorial demonstrates building a production-style multi-agent travel planning application using LangGraph for agent orchestration and MCP servers for real-time data integration. The system connects flight and weather data APIs through MCP, stores long-term memory in PostgreSQL, and provides both terminal and Streamlit interfaces.
writeup: Part 2 of this tutorial builds a multi-agent travel planning system that connects LangGraph agents to MCP servers for real-time flight and weather data. It wires together concrete components: PostgreSQL for agent memory, Tavily Search, and the AviationStack and OpenWeatherMap APIs. The build ships with two interfaces, a terminal client and a Streamlit app. It demonstrates how the Model Context Protocol coordinates multiple agents around live external data rather than static prompts.
